# grafana_assistant_rule (Resource)

Manages a Grafana Assistant rule that is injected into the assistant system prompt.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "grafana_assistant_rule" "example" {
  name         = "Prefer RED metrics"
  rule_content = "When summarizing service health, prefer RED metrics."
  scope        = "tenant"
  priority     = 10
  applications = ["assistant"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The rule name.
- `rule_content` (String) The rule text included in the assistant system prompt.
- `scope` (String) Whether the resource is visible to the whole tenant (`tenant`) or only the creating user (`user`).

### Optional

- `applications` (List of String) Applications where this resource applies. Valid values: `assistant`, `loop`, `infrastructure_memory` (rules only), `all`. Defaults to all applications when unset.
- `description` (String) Optional description of the rule.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the resource is enabled.
- `priority` (Number) Rule priority (lower values apply first).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The UUID of the resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
terraform import grafana_assistant_rule.name "{{ id }}"
```
